#AI needs power.

A lot of it.

And the buildout to feed that demand runs through #nuclear (#uranium), energy storage, grid expansion and semiconductors.

There is one surprisingly overlooked link between them:

#Fluorspar.

Fluorspar is the only commercial source of fluorine. Uranium enrichment depends on UF₆. EV batteries can require 5–10x more fluorspar than lithium. Advanced chip manufacturing relies on fluorine chemistry.

Yet the U.S. produces zero primary fluorspar.

CleanTech Vanadium $CTV.V @CleanTechCTV is now moving beyond land assembly and into permitting and drilling in the historic Illinois-Kentucky Fluorspar District.

tweets.text:
New blog post focused on a fun paper by @danivilardell77 and @sjbreck

They study voter privacy via bribery games and call it B-Privacy.     

Why?     

Privacy in weighted voting is harder than it looks.  

Even if every ballot is encrypted, the final tally itself can leak how people voted.    

Worse: that leakage can make vote buying enfoceable   

It is not straight forward to make a weighted voting system private without hiding the tally.    

So, can you do something in between, where there is "enough" voting privacy such that the briber's cost is increased by several multiples?     

Find out in the next tweet 👇
